title: Set up Neon Serverless Postgres in Next.js
summary: Connect Next.js to Neon's serverless Postgres using the edge-compatible driver with Drizzle ORM for type-safe queries.
declaration: I will connect this Next.js project to Neon Serverless Postgres using @neondatabase/serverless and Drizzle ORM. You will need a free Neon account (neon.tech) and a DATABASE_URL connection string. If you prefer Prisma over Drizzle, say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Set up Neon Serverless Postgres in Next.js Neon is a serverless Postgres provider with a generous free tier and auto-suspend that is ideal for Next.js. Its WebSocket-based driver works in both serverless and Edge runtimes. ### 1. Create a Neon project - Go to neon.tech and sign up for a free account. - Create a new project and choose a region close to your deployment target. - From the Neon dashboard, copy the **pooled** connection string. It looks like: `postgresql://user:pass@ep-xxx.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech/dbname?sslmode=require` - Add it to `.env.local`: ``` DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:pass@ep-xxx.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech/dbname?sslmode=require" ``` - Neon also provides an **unpooled** string — save that separately for running migrations. ### 2. Install packages - Run: `npm install @neondatabase/serverless drizzle-orm` - Run: `npm install -D drizzle-kit` - `@neondatabase/serverless` is the edge-compatible driver — it works in Node.js, Edge Runtime, and Cloudflare Workers. ### 3. Create the database client - Create `src/db/index.ts`: ```typescript import { neon } from '@neondatabase/serverless'; import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/neon-http'; const sql = neon(process.env.DATABASE_URL!); export const db = drizzle(sql); ``` - Import `db` from this file anywhere you need to query the database. ### 4. Define your schema - Create `src/db/schema.ts`: ```typescript import { pgTable, serial, text, varchar, timestamp } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core'; export const posts = pgTable('posts', { id: serial('id').primaryKey(), title: varchar('title', { length: 256 }).notNull(), content: text('content'), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow().notNull(), }); export type Post = typeof posts.$inferSelect; export type NewPost = typeof posts.$inferInsert; ``` ### 5. Configure Drizzle Kit - Create `drizzle.config.ts` in the project root: ```typescript import type { Config } from 'drizzle-kit'; export default { schema: './src/db/schema.ts', out: './drizzle', dialect: 'postgresql', dbCredentials: { url: process.env.DATABASE_URL! }, } satisfies Config; ``` ### 6. Add migration scripts to package.json - Add to the `scripts` section: ```json { "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate", "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate", "db:push": "drizzle-kit push", "db:studio": "drizzle-kit studio" } ``` - For development: `npm run db:push` syncs schema without creating migration files. - For production: `npm run db:generate` then `npm run db:migrate` for version-controlled migrations. ### 7. Push or migrate the schema - Ensure `DATABASE_URL` is exported: `export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..."` - Development: `npm run db:push` - Verify: Run `npm run db:studio` to open Drizzle Studio and inspect your tables. - For migrations, use the **unpooled** connection string (pgBouncer does not support DDL statements). ### 8. Query in Server Components ```typescript import { db } from '@/db'; import { posts } from '@/db/schema'; export default async function PostsPage() { const allPosts = await db.select().from(posts); return (
    {allPosts.map((post) => (
  • {post.title}
    • ))}
); } ``` ### 9. Write in Server Actions - Create `src/actions/posts.ts`: ```typescript 'use server'; import { db } from '@/db'; import { posts } from '@/db/schema'; import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'; export async function createPost(title: string, content: string) { await db.insert(posts).values({ title, content }); revalidatePath('/posts'); } ``` ### 10. Set environment variables in production - For Vercel: add `DATABASE_URL` in Settings > Environment Variables. - Always use the **pooled** connection string for API routes and Server Actions. - Use the **unpooled** string only for running `drizzle-kit migrate`. ### 11. Verify the setup - Start the dev server: `npm run dev`. - Navigate to a page that queries the database and confirm data renders. - Check the Neon dashboard — you should see query activity in the Monitoring tab. ### Common errors & fixes - **'fetch failed' or 'ECONNREFUSED'** — `DATABASE_URL` is wrong or not set. Double-check `.env.local` and restart the dev server. - **'SSL SYSCALL error: EOF detected'** — Missing `?sslmode=require` at the end of your connection string. Neon requires SSL. - **First query takes ~500ms** — Neon auto-suspends after 5 minutes of inactivity on the free tier. The cold-start delay is expected. - **'drizzle-kit: command not found'** — Install as a dev dependency: `npm install -D drizzle-kit`. - **Migration fails with 'permission denied'** — You are using the pooled string for migrations. Switch to the unpooled connection string for `drizzle-kit migrate`. - **Type errors on `db.select()`** — Ensure `drizzle-orm` and `drizzle-kit` versions are compatible. Run `npm ls drizzle-orm drizzle-kit` to verify. - **`process.env.DATABASE_URL` is undefined in production** — Add the env var in your deployment platform's dashboard; `.env.local` is not read in production. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT use the `pg` or `postgres` npm packages directly with Neon — they do not support WebSocket connections and fail in Edge/serverless environments. Always use `@neondatabase/serverless`. - Do NOT commit `DATABASE_URL` to Git — add `.env.local` to `.gitignore`. - Do NOT use the pooled connection string for `drizzle-kit migrate` — pgBouncer blocks DDL. Use the unpooled string for all schema operations. - Do NOT query the database inside Client Components — only query in Server Components, Server Actions, or Route Handlers. - Do NOT create a new `neon()` client per request in a long-lived server — reuse the singleton exported from `src/db/index.ts`. - Do NOT run `drizzle-kit push` in production — use `generate` + `migrate` for reproducible, auditable schema changes.
